section: The World of Metals and Non-Metals (Mixed Difficulty)

questions.question: Which two metals are so soft they can be cut with a knife?
questions.explanation: Unlike most hard metals, these are soft and also highly reactive with air and water.

questions.question: What happens when a copper plate is exposed to moist air for a long time?
questions.correct_answer: It develops a dull green coating
questions.explanation: The green material is a mixture of copper hydroxide and copper carbonate, formed by reaction with moisture and $CO_2$.

questions.question: Explain the 'Displacement Reaction' using Zinc and Copper Sulphate.
questions.correct_answer: Zinc replaces Copper to form Zinc Sulphate and Copper metal
questions.explanation: Zinc is more reactive than copper. The blue color of copper sulphate fades and a red mass of copper is deposited.

questions.question: Which non-metal is stored in water to prevent it from catching fire?
questions.explanation: Phosphorus is very reactive; it catches fire if exposed to air, so it is kept submerged in water.

questions.question: What is the nature of the oxide formed by burning a piece of Sulphur?
questions.explanation: Sulphur dioxide gas dissolved in water forms sulphurous acid, which turns blue litmus red.

questions.question: Why is Graphite used in pencil leads despite being a non-metal?
questions.correct_answer: It is soft, leaves a mark on paper, and is a form of Carbon
questions.explanation: Graphite is an exception among non-metals as it also conducts electricity.

questions.question: What gas is produced when metals react with dilute acids?
questions.explanation: The presence of hydrogen is confirmed by the 'pop' sound when a burning matchstick is brought near it.

questions.question: Why can a copper wire not be used to displace iron from iron sulphate solution?
questions.correct_answer: Because Copper is less reactive than Iron
questions.explanation: A less reactive metal cannot displace a more reactive metal from its salt solution.

questions.question: Which non-metal is used in the purple-colored solution applied on wounds as an antiseptic?
questions.explanation: Tincture of iodine is a common antiseptic used to treat cuts and wounds.

questions.question: Explain why Aluminum foils are used to wrap food items.
questions.explanation: It can be beaten into very thin sheets that are safe and effective for keeping food fresh.

notes.title: Reactivity Series
notes.content: Metals are arranged in a series based on their reactivity. Higher metals displace lower ones.

notes.title: Rusting Formula
notes.content: Iron + Oxygen + Water → Iron oxide (Fe₂O₃ · xH₂O), commonly known as rust.

notes.title: Non-metal Conductivity
notes.content: Most non-metals are poor conductors of heat and electricity, except for Graphite.

facts.fact: Sodium is stored in kerosene because it reacts vigorously with oxygen and water.
Silver and Gold do not react with water or oxygen even at high temperatures.
Magnesium burns in air with a dazzling white flame to form Magnesium Oxide.
